author: @albertmueller6616
text: I don't know how many of you have tried to turn on the closed caption for this video but I DID. Let me tell you people, If you want a good "side splitting laugh" turn it on and watch. Its absolutely hilarious what it thinks he is saying.

author: @dreven777
text: Please add a sponge-y material around the microphone, like cut a hole in a little sponge and stick it in. That will fix most of the echo problem. And please, you are using mono microphone, so people hear only left speaker or headphone, you can copy the same sound track to the right channel too, so it is stereo. If you cannot do this, please get a another mic! Get some tech-y kids around you to help!

author: @scantrain5007
text:
Dear Patty and Robert, 
it's a great idea to offer the Battry Kit. 
I tried already to buy some parts by myself - a nightmare! I simpy failed at the beinning when I bought activated carbon by Boots: Very low conductivity. Not talking about binder, small amount of Zinc and so on. 
A little few hints for future Reserchers: If buying a multimeter, try to get one with USB-Connection for recording on Computer. A Chinese Company offers Battery Tester as well where you can make diagrams on Computer. Both are less than 120 Quid; so not a big deal if you relly like to compare your own made different types of batteries or supercaps.  
Hope you have a lot of fun doing the seminar! It's a pitty that I cannot be there ...

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Thanks for the comment mate

author: @ancapftw9113
text: Have you considered using a wireless wearable microphone? The echo make it kind of hard to understand you.

replies.author: @rickdees251
replies.text: He's like the British are with their teeth. His audio quality is not Hollywood quality. Sometimes it's a stopper to hearing all that is said and sometimes it's not. It really frustrating when it's not. It's what we get.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Tried that - had even more complaints

replies.author: @truetech4158
replies.text:
AncapFTW people have grown used to the moderate echo. And in fact, it is understood that the echo is actually something unique to Robert's voice. It's not the surroundings that cause that, its really because his voice has a most unique naturally occurring echo. Interestingly enough, it occurs even when he speaks while inside a soundproofed studio and all other locations as well.
Perfect if he were to be in a rock band that requires no additional effects.

replies.author: @ZeroInDaHouse
replies.text: the microphone problem has sort of become the running joke. The day it gets fixed we will never here from Robert again, wouldn't that be ironic :).

replies.author: @RWBHere
replies.text:
Robert probably tried putting the carpet onto the ceiling, but then had problems with echoes from the floor....

A microphone closer to his mouth would be the obvious answer to the perceived problem.

OTOH, for the most part I don't even notice the audio deficiencies, and accept the sound as it stands. After a few seconds, the brain filters out the noise, and his words become understandable. Patti is harder to hear than Robert, at first, but again, her words become clear after a few seconds of adjustment. 

I'm only using an old iPad for these videos, so I never hear any of the Bass notes in their voices. Maybe that's an advantage?
